title: podman machine provider parameter
body:
### Feature request description

Currently you can customize the provider by using for example
```
export CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER="applehv"
```
or
```
$env:CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER="hyperv"
```

### Suggest potential solution

I would expect that the provider a first class citizen option and to be able to provide it through a parameter

`podman machine init --provider applehv`

### Have you considered any alternatives?

using environment options is not very straightforward for users

### Additional context

I expect that I should receive all available providers if I don't specify one

comments.body:
This is not really a good idea with the current machine design. Each provider uses separates configs and knows nothing about each other basically. This means that if you would use a non default provider you would need to provider flag for each machine command and the user would need to specify the option every time which is clearly not a good design.

I am not sure if @baude is planing to address this with the rework for podman 5.0.

From user point of view, it's not convenient to add environment variable on each command you want to run or need to export some environment variable

I expect also to see all my machines (whatever provider being set) if I list them.

else, I would need to go through to several list commands to see if I have one or not and as I don't know the name of the providers, it's like a dead end.

You can configure the provider in containers.conf.
I am not saying your suggestion is bad idea, I think it totally makes sense but unless we completely rework all the machine backend code this is not going to work properly.
I know @baude is planning to rework machine code for 5.0 I just do not know if this would be part of it.
In Podman 5, using containers.conf is the preferred approach.
this the preferred approach but users can still create machines using different providers and env variable is still supported

Hi all > In Podman 5, using containers.conf is the preferred approach. Where is containers.conf locate on macOS Big Sure 11.7.10? I'm attempting to try a different vm provider because I'm getting an error when running: `podman machine init` The error is: **`Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 1`** I'm not sure changing the provider will help but I figured I'd give it a shot because I have not been able to find a solution through Google. My _vfkit_ version is: `vfkit version: 0.5.1` I tried changing the provider to **qemu** using the environment variable: CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER but that came back with an error when trying to list the machines: ``` elliot@Elliot-Mac-BigSur:~$ podman machine list Error: unsupported virtualization provider: `qemu` ``` Also I ran `podman machine reset` - in hopes that would solve the issue but it did not. Also I apologize if this is the wrong forum for my issue. E.

> macOS Big Sur 11.7.10?

This is an older macos version which won't work with the podman vfkit provider.

Ah ok Christophe, thank you so much for the reply.

Is it possible for me to use "another" provider? Or is my only option to
use an older version of Podman?

Sincere Thanks for any help you can provide.

E.

Elliot Holden
+1 706-231-9776 (Cell and WhatsApp)
+1 404-500-6577 (Google Voice)
www.ElliotHolden.com <http://www.elliotholden.com>

Hey Christophe,

I downgraded from Podman 5.2.2 and installed Podman 4.9.5 (which uses **qemu** as the default provider) on my macOS Big Sur 11.7.10, , and everything is working now. Thanks again for your reply and setting me on the right path.

E.

@Luap99 I'm curious if there is a chance to push this idea a bit forward and implement `--provider` tag. The reason:
- Recent Podman Desktop changed the default provider on MacOS from Apple Hypervisor to LibKrun
- This breaks permissions (not sure if it's a real issue or I have to do more work). For example this will break with permissions issues:

```yaml
mkdir .devcontainer
cat <<'EOF' > .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
{
  "image": "mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.11"
}
EOF
code .
touch abc  # in devcontainer
```

- The easiest fix is switching to Podman Machine with Apple Hypervisor. However I just came from a call where a user had no toggle to switch provider tab visible in the GUI (as well as rootful / rootless). The installation details remain unknown though.
- I understand the right way will be to use `containers.conf`, but explaining all that to the user makes like harder. =)
- We used the env variable, but --provider knob will be much appreciated for this case.

This was already fixed for 6.0 in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27301 and https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27386

Note that podman 6 also switches to libkrun as default.

If you have a problem with permissions you need to file a new issue with a proper information (what are the permissions outside/inside as what user runs the machine and container, etc...) and ideally reproduce this without devcontainer as we don't really know what this is doing.
